requirements.name: Autonomous Agent Task Execution
requirements.description: AI agents check out assigned tasks and execute them end-to-end (write code, conduct research, produce documents) with full run audit trail tracking.

requirements.name: Human-in-the-Loop Governance
requirements.description: Approval gates for high-stakes actions. Humans can review, comment, approve, or reject before agents proceed. Agents block cleanly and resume on approval.

requirements.name: Agentic Hierarchy & Delegation
requirements.description: Define agent roles (CEO, Engineer, PM). Managers delegate subtasks to reports with automatic parent-child linkage and budget propagation.

requirements.name: Full Issue Lifecycle Management
requirements.description: CRUD on issues with structured status transitions (todo \u2192 in_progress \u2192 blocked \u2192 review \u2192 done), priority, assignment, and goal alignment.

requirements.name: Run Observability Dashboard
requirements.description: Every agent action is logged to a heartbeat run. Board members inspect what happened, why, what it cost, and link runs to issues for accountability.

requirements.name: Stateless Agent Architecture
requirements.description: Agents do not hold state between heartbeats. All state lives in platform DB. Makes agents interchangeable and failure-safe.

requirements.name: Run-ID Tracing
requirements.description: Every mutating API call includes X-Paperclip-Run-Id header. Links every change to auditable heartbeat run enabling full replay and forensic analysis.

requirements.name: Chain of Command Escalation
requirements.description: Agents have reportsTo hierarchy. When blocked, agent escalates to manager. Platform enforces this; cross-team delegation requires billingCode.

requirements.name: Budget Control & Enforcement
requirements.description: Monthly spend tracked per agent in cents. Platform auto-pauses agents at 100% budget. Above 80%, only critical tasks execute.

requirements.name: Approval Gates for Irreversible Actions
requirements.description: Actions with high blast radius (create agents, push to prod, delete data) must go through approval workflow. Approvals linked to issues for full context.

requirements.name: Adapter Abstraction Layer
requirements.description: Agents are model-agnostic. Adapter is subprocess contract: receive env vars, do work, exit. claude_local spawns claude CLI; codex_local spawns codex. New adapters are additive.

requirements.name: REST API \u2014 Issues Management
requirements.description: GET/POST /api/companies/:id/issues for list and create. Full CRUD with status/field updates and inline comments.

requirements.name: Atomic Checkout Protocol
requirements.description: POST /api/issues/:id/checkout implements compare-and-swap to prevent concurrent assignment. Returns 409 Conflict if already checked out.

requirements.name: Agent Service & Heartbeat Scheduling
requirements.description: Agent runtime polls heartbeats, injects PAPERCLIP_* env vars, invokes adapter subprocess, tracks run lifecycle (queued \u2192 running \u2192 completed/failed).

requirements.name: Governance Service \u2014 Approval Workflows
requirements.description: Enforces company policy at runtime with approval gates for privileged actions and escalation chains derived from chainOfCommand.

requirements.name: Immutable Audit Log
requirements.description: Append-only audit log for all state mutations. No DELETE endpoint exists for audit records.

requirements.name: PostgreSQL Data Model
requirements.description: Core entities: Company, Agent, Issue, AgentRun, Project, Goal, Approval, AuditLog with structured schema and relationships.

requirements.name: JWT Authentication & Authorization
requirements.description: All API requests authenticated via JWT. Agents receive short-lived run JWTs scoped to single run. Board users have persistent JWTs with role-based permissions.

requirements.name: Row-Level Tenant Isolation
requirements.description: No agent can access another company's data. Enforced at database level.

requirements.name: Sensitive Data Protection
requirements.description: Sensitive env vars (API keys, DB credentials) never logged.

requirements.name: Core Package Architecture
requirements.description: Four packages: @agentic/core (types, models, errors), @agentic/api (REST API), @agentic/agent-runtime (scheduler + adapter), @agentic/governance (approvals, audit, budget).

requirements.name: TypeScript Strict Mode & ESM
requirements.description: Strict TypeScript mode enforced with exactOptionalPropertyTypes and noUncheckedIndexedAccess. ESM modules only with .js extensions.

requirements.name: Co-located Unit Tests
requirements.description: Tests live next to files they test (foo.test.ts beside foo.ts). Uses Node's built-in test runner. All new logic requires tests.

requirements.name: Drizzle ORM & Migrations
requirements.description: All schema changes in packages/api/src/db/schema.ts. Migrations generated with npm run db:generate. Never raw SQL in application code.

requirements.name: Trunk-Based Git Workflow
requirements.description: main branch always deployable. Feature branches off main named <type>/<short-description>. Squash merge to keep history clean. Delete after merge.

requirements.name: Conventional Commits
requirements.description: Commit messages follow Conventional Commits: feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, test:, refactor:. AI agents append Co-Authored-By: Paperclip.

requirements.name: CI/CD Pipeline \u2014 GitHub Actions
requirements.description: Every PR runs: npm run lint (TypeScript check), npm run build (compile), npm run test (tests), npm audit (vulnerability scan). Deploy to staging on main merge.

requirements.name: Product Vision & Market Positioning
requirements.description: Target founding teams 2-20 engineers. Solve 30-40% PM overhead. AI agents execute autonomously with human governance and audit trails.

requirements.name: MVP Success Metrics
requirements.description: Activation: 3+ issues completed in 7 days. Retention: \u226560% run \u22655 heartbeats/week by day 30. Quality: <5% rollback rate. Revenue: first paying customer by day 60.

requirements.name: GROOT v3.1 Lifecycle Engine
requirements.description: 7-phase lifecycle, 10 role archetypes, parallel tracks, worker packets (17 fields), model routing (3 tiers per task), handoff contracts, board gates, auto-heal mechanics.

requirements.name: Oracle Layer \u2014 3-Tier Intelligence
requirements.description: Main Oracle (global), Project Oracle (per-project context), Data Guy (session intelligence). Agents query live context instead of carrying stale copies.

requirements.name: Main Oracle \u2014 Global Intelligence
requirements.description: Aggregates all Project Oracles. Sees patterns, cross-project dependencies, resource conflicts, systemic risks. Pre-populates new Project Oracles from institutional knowledge.

requirements.name: Project Oracle \u2014 Per-Project Context
requirements.description: One per active project. Loaded with PRD, architecture, tasks, agent assignments, dependencies, blockers, risks. Agents query instead of carrying context. Retires with project but data archived.

requirements.name: Data Guy \u2014 Session Intelligence
requirements.description: Watches real-time: files touched, errors, decisions, task state. Writes session summary on close \u2192 feeds Project Oracle. Lightweight, always-on, never expensive.

requirements.name: Superpowers \u2014 Quality Discipline
requirements.description: Spec review gates (up to 5 iterations), plan review before code, TDD enforcement (red\u2192green\u2192refactor), evidence-based completion, code review agent, git worktree isolation.

requirements.name: Jonah's Critique \u2014 Operational Controls
requirements.description: Formal baselines, hard change control, named authorities, budget/variance tracking, configuration management, test entry/exit criteria, defect triage, 508 compliance gate.

requirements.name: OpenClaw Agent Network Integration
requirements.description: DX Framework wraps 22 OpenClaw agents. Framework tells what to do; OpenClaw makes it happen via multi-model routing, session isolation, credential management.

requirements.name: 22 Agent Roster
requirements.description: CEO, Brain, Forge, Oracle, Cipher, and 17 more specialized agents for development, testing, documentation, integration, and domain-specific tasks.

requirements.name: 7-Phase Project Lifecycle
requirements.description: Seven phases orchestrated by GROOT: requirement\u2192design\u2192architecture\u2192implementation\u2192testing\u2192deployment\u2192monitoring. Each with phase gates and worker packets.

requirements.name: Worker Packets \u2014 17-Field Task Format
requirements.description: Standardized task format with 17 fields. Routes tasks through model tiers (3 per task). Enables seamless handoff between agents and phase gates.

requirements.name: Parallel Track Management
requirements.description: Multiple workstreams run in parallel. Dependency graph ensures blockers are detected. Work packets route through correct agents based on phase and track.

requirements.name: Board Gates & Phase Control
requirements.description: Human approval gates at critical phases. Board reviews baseline, PRD, architecture, release readiness. Everything else delegated to agents.

requirements.name: Human Command Authority Layer
requirements.description: YOU set goals, approve baselines, make final release decisions, review board gates. Direction, not execution. Everything else delegated downward.

requirements.name: Cost Control & Variance Tracking
requirements.description: Budget management per agent role. Variance tracking with alerts. Cost visibility per project and phase. Named cost authority reviews.

requirements.name: TDD Enforcement
requirements.description: Red\u2192Green\u2192Refactor cycle enforced. Tests must pass for code completion. Evidence-based completion, not agent-claimed done.

requirements.name: Specification Review Gates
requirements.description: Up to 5 specification iteration cycles. PRD and architecture reviewed before code starts. Prevents rework.

requirements.name: Git Worktree Isolation
requirements.description: Each task isolated in git worktree. Clean dependency isolation, prevents merge conflicts, enables parallel work without interference.

requirements.name: Code Review Agent
requirements.description: Dedicated agent for code review. Enforces quality standards, catches architectural issues, ensures consistency before human review.

requirements.name: Configuration Baselines
requirements.description: Formal baselines for each phase: requirements baseline, design baseline, build baseline, release baseline. Change control gates for each.

requirements.name: Hard Change Control
requirements.description: Named change authority. Formal impact analysis. Configuration item tracking. Prevents scope creep and chaotic changes.

requirements.name: Test Entry/Exit Criteria
requirements.description: Formal entry criteria (code ready, env stable) and exit criteria (coverage met, no critical bugs) for testing phases.

requirements.name: Defect Triage Model
requirements.description: Severity/priority matrix for defects. Triage rules determine fix/defer decisions. Blocks release on critical issues.

requirements.name: 508 Compliance Gate
requirements.description: Accessibility compliance verification. Blocks release if 508/WCAG violations found.

requirements.name: Auto-Heal Mechanics
requirements.description: GROOT lifecycle auto-detects failures and routes to recovery agents. Reduces manual intervention.

requirements.name: Hive Mind Module
requirements.description: Agent collective intelligence. Cross-agent knowledge sharing and pattern detection.

requirements.name: Darwin Module
requirements.description: Evolutionary task optimization. Learns from prior runs to improve execution efficiency.

requirements.name: Model Routing \u2014 3-Tier Strategy
requirements.description: Each task routed through 3 model tiers. Enables cost optimization and quality matching.

requirements.name: Handoff Contracts
requirements.description: Structured contracts between agents on handoff. Defines input, output, quality criteria, escalation paths.

requirements.name: Role Archetype Library
requirements.description: 10 role archetypes: Architect, Developer, Tester, PM, BA, QA, DevOps, Security, Docs, Integration. Defined responsibilities and handoff points.

requirements.name: Task Queue Visualization

requirements.name: System Health Alerts

requirements.name: Agent Performance Analytics

requirements.name: Multi-environment Support

requirements.name: Unified Activity Feed

requirements.name: Cost Attribution Dashboard
requirements.description: Break down API costs, compute costs, and token usage by project, agent, and task. Month-over-month comparison with budget forecasting. CSV export.

requirements.name: Codebase Intelligence Panel
requirements.description: Visual map of codebase health: test coverage, tech debt score, dependency age, security audit status. Drill-down to file-level detail with change history.

requirements.name: Developer Velocity Metrics
requirements.description: Track lines changed, PRs merged, review turnaround, and deployment frequency. Team and individual views with customizable date ranges. Integration with GitHub.

requirements.name: Onboarding Flow
requirements.description: Step-by-step onboarding for new team members. Auto-detects tools, configures integrations, and generates a personalized setup checklist. Progress persistence.

requirements.name: Notification Preferences
requirements.description: Granular notification settings per event type, per channel (email, Slack, SMS, push). Quiet hours, digest mode, and per-project overrides.

requirements.name: Orchestration Engine
requirements.description: DAG-based task orchestration supporting parallel and sequential workflows. Visual workflow builder with conditional branching, retry logic, and timeout handling.

requirements.name: Command History & Replay
requirements.description: Full audit trail of all commands issued through the system. Point-in-time replay capability for debugging. Searchable with full-text and filter support.

requirements.name: Permission Governance Layer
requirements.description: Role-based access control for command execution. Approval workflows for high-impact operations. Temporary elevated access with automatic expiry. SSO integration.

requirements.name: Cross-Agent Communication Bus
requirements.description: Message broker enabling reliable inter-agent communication. Pub/sub and request/reply patterns. Message persistence, dead-letter queue, and replay.

requirements.name: Scenario Simulation Mode
requirements.description: Run commands in a sandboxed dry-run mode that predicts outcomes without executing. Shows diff of what would change. Critical for testing complex orchestrations.

requirements.name: Agent SDK Core
requirements.description: Base SDK providing lifecycle hooks, memory interfaces, tool registration, and communication primitives. TypeScript and Python. Comprehensive documentation with examples.

requirements.name: Memory Management System
requirements.description: Tiered memory: short-term (context window), working (session), long-term (persistent). Automatic summarization and compression. Vector search for semantic recall.

requirements.name: Tool Plugin Architecture
requirements.description: Extensible tool system allowing agents to discover and use external capabilities. Tool versioning, capability declarations, and sandboxed execution. Marketplace for sharing.

requirements.name: Agent Testing Framework
requirements.description: Unit and integration testing for agent behaviors. Mock tool responses, scenario injection, and behavioral assertions. CI/CD integration with GitHub Actions.

requirements.name: Multi-Modal Input Support
requirements.description: Enable agents to process images, audio, and documents alongside text. Standardized input adapters with preprocessing pipelines. Format conversion utilities.

requirements.name: Agent Marketplace
requirements.description: Publish, discover, and deploy pre-built agents. Versioning, ratings, usage stats. One-click deployment with configuration templates.

requirements.name: Contextual Conversation Memory
requirements.description: Remember user preferences, ongoing projects, and past conversations per Discord user/server. Context persists across sessions with configurable retention periods.

requirements.name: Slash Command Framework
requirements.description: Extensible slash command system with auto-complete, parameter validation, and ephemeral/public responses. Command groups, permissions, and usage analytics.

requirements.name: Knowledge Base Integration
requirements.description: Index and query internal documentation, Notion pages, and GitHub wikis. RAG pipeline for accurate, cited responses. Auto-sync on document updates.

requirements.name: Automated Meeting Summaries
requirements.description: Join voice channels, transcribe conversations, and post structured summaries with action items and decisions to designated channels. Speaker identification.

requirements.name: Multi-Server Governance
requirements.description: Manage bot permissions and behaviors across multiple Discord servers from a single admin interface. Per-server feature flags and usage quotas.

requirements.name: API Gateway Core
requirements.description: Central gateway for routing, rate limiting, authentication, and caching external API calls. Circuit breakers, retry logic, and failover. OpenAPI spec auto-import.

requirements.name: Credential Vault
requirements.description: Secure storage for API keys, OAuth tokens, and secrets. Automatic rotation policies, expiry alerts, and audit trail. Envelope encryption with KMS.

requirements.name: Webhook Manager
requirements.description: Register, test, and monitor inbound webhooks. Payload validation, retry with exponential backoff, dead-letter queue. Visual event inspector.

requirements.name: API Transformation Pipeline
requirements.description: Visual pipeline builder for transforming request/response payloads. JSONata expressions, field mapping, and conditional routing. Reusable transformation templates.

requirements.name: Usage Analytics & Billing
requirements.description: Detailed API usage metrics per endpoint, consumer, and time period. Cost attribution, anomaly detection, and quota enforcement with soft and hard limits.

requirements.name: API Version Management
requirements.description: Manage multiple API versions with deprecation timelines and migration guides. Automated compatibility checks between versions. Consumer notification system.

requirements.name: Head of Product Role
requirements.description: Hire a product-minded analyst to own the product roadmap, customer feedback synthesis, and feature specifications. Will own the full sales assist and demo story from a product perspective. Responsible for structured discovery post-MVP, customer interview synthesis, acceptance criteria, and competitive intelligence.

requirements.name: Head of Growth/GTM Role
requirements.description: Hire SDR/AE hybrid to own first customer acquisition pipeline: outbound targeting, demo scheduling, objection handling, and initial onboarding handoff. Must start before MVP completion to build pipeline for Day 60 paying customer target. Hands deals to CEO for final close.

requirements.name: Product Ownership Void Gap
requirements.description: Current gap: no one owns customer-to-roadmap pipeline. CEO handles strategy; FE handles execution. Missing: user research synthesis, feature prioritization, acceptance criteria, roadmap sequencing. Every product decision escalates to CEO, creating ~40% strategic bandwidth bottleneck.

requirements.name: No GTM / First Customer Path Gap
requirements.description: Success metric is a paying customer by Day 60, but company has zero GTM capacity: no outbound, no demos, no pipeline. Pipeline takes 4-6 weeks to build. Starting GTM hire after MVP launch is too late. Must begin hiring before MVP completion.

requirements.name: Ethical & Legal Compliance Unowned Gap
requirements.description: Company mission explicitly names 'ethical and legal automation' but no one owns governance documentation, legal review of agent permissions, or compliance posture. AI agents taking real actions is trust-sensitive. Missing governance creates reputational and legal risk as company signs first customers.

requirements.name: No Design/UX Voice Gap
requirements.description: Observability dashboard and governance UX are primary trust interfaces with users. First impressions set by how well humans can read agent actions and reasoning. Design quality currently depends entirely on FE shipping; no dedicated UX function.

requirements.name: Agent Budget Allocation Policy
requirements.description: Agent monthly budgets currently set to $0. Board must establish per-role budget caps before approving Head of Product and Head of Growth hires. Both new roles will require model budget to operate.

requirements.name: Rhabdomyolysis Root Cause Analysis
requirements.description: Dr. Al must conduct comprehensive analysis of Kevin's rhabdomyolysis episodes (Aug 2025 CK >22,000, Jan 2024 CK ~10,000) including risk assessment, prevention strategies, and connection to genetic variants (AGK c.445A>G, AGK c.764C>T, AMPD1 c.133C>T).

requirements.name: Genetic Variant Analysis - AMPD1 & AGK
requirements.description: Deep genomic research into Kevin's pathogenic variants (AGK c.445A>G p.Ile149Val, AGK c.764C>T p.Thr255Met, AMPD1 c.133C>T p.Gln45*) including metabolic myopathy implications, inheritance patterns, and pathogenic mechanisms.

requirements.name: Supplement Stack Optimization
requirements.description: Identify and optimize supplement protocols to support Kevin's metabolic condition, manage CK elevation, support energy metabolism, and complement medical interventions.

requirements.name: CK Monitoring Protocol Development
requirements.description: Create comprehensive CK (creatine kinase) monitoring and management protocol including test frequency, threshold triggers, interventions, and integration with supplement/lifestyle protocols.

requirements.name: Weekly Health Status Review
requirements.description: Conduct weekly health briefings with Kevin covering research findings, biomarker trends, supplement efficacy, symptom patterns, and treatment recommendations.

requirements.name: Multi-Specialist Health Team Coordination
requirements.description: Coordinate specialist consultations including core team (Neurologist, Geneticist, Nutritionist) and on-demand specialists (Cardiologist, Rheumatologist, Endocrinologist, etc.) with holistic and emerging medicine options.

requirements.name: Medical Malpractice & Negligence Litigation Support
requirements.description: Provide litigation support for medical malpractice cases including standards of care analysis, deviation identification, causation proof, and expert witness coordination. Dirty Bird has first-chair jury trial experience in complex medical cases.

requirements.name: Federal Whistleblower Protection & OSC Complaint Support
requirements.description: Handle Whistleblower Protection Act (WPA) and Office of Special Counsel (OSC) complaint processes, federal retaliation claims, protected disclosure linkage, and agency defense navigation.

requirements.name: Data Breach & Privacy Litigation
requirements.description: Manage data breach and privacy litigation involving FISMA, Privacy Act, federal data protection standards, unauthorized access claims, and system-level evidence reconstruction.

requirements.name: Regulatory & Compliance Violation Analysis
requirements.description: Analyze regulatory compliance violations including IRM, Treasury regulations, internal control frameworks, systemic failures vs. isolated incidents, and TIGTA escalation pathways.

requirements.name: Evidence Preservation & Case Timeline Reconstruction
requirements.description: Manage evidence preservation, spoliation arguments, timeline reconstruction across systems/emails/personnel, and documentation strategy for airtight case narratives.

requirements.name: Expert Witness Strategy & Network
requirements.description: Coordinate expert witness strategy including established network of medical, forensic, and IT/security experts; dismantling opposing expert opinions; and case strength optimization.

requirements.name: Government Defense & Federal Court Navigation
requirements.description: Navigate federal court procedures, sovereign immunity limitations, jurisdictional strategy, and litigation against federal agencies and institutional defendants.

requirements.name: Contract Review & Drafting
requirements.description: Comprehensive contract review, drafting, risk mitigation, and negotiation support across all business agreements.

requirements.name: Intellectual Property Protection
requirements.description: IP protection strategy including trademarks, copyrights, patents, licensing agreements, and enforcement actions.

requirements.name: Continuous Compliance Monitoring
requirements.description: Ongoing monitoring of regulatory changes, compliance gaps, tax obligations, labor law updates, and business license/permit requirements with proactive issue flagging.

requirements.name: Legal Risk Assessment & Threat Detection
requirements.description: Identify malpractice liability threats, legal exposure from business decisions, litigation risk assessment, and proactive risk mitigation recommendations.

requirements.name: Business Structure Optimization
requirements.description: Provide recommendations for business entity structure, tax optimization, liability protection, governance setup, and organizational changes to support growth.

requirements.name: OpenClaw Migration Framework Documentation
requirements.description: Comprehensive guide for migrating OpenClaw setup from existing user account to new user account on Mac Mini. Includes phases for preparation, data transfer, and target account setup with launchd service configuration.

requirements.name: GROOT Agentic Project Lifecycle Framework - Modular v3.1
requirements.description: Fully namespaced, OpenClaw-compatible HTML framework for agentic project lifecycle management with comprehensive theme system (dark-command, dark-void, mid-slate, mid-espresso, light-arctic, light-parchment), spectarget themes, and design tokens. Includes modular components and CSS system.

requirements.name: GROOT Design System Template
requirements.description: Reusable design system template v3.1 for building GROOT-compatible applications. Includes comprehensive theme definitions, typography scale, component library templates, and CSS reset utilities for drop-in component development.

requirements.name: Mission Control v3 - Kanban Board (2D Dashboard)
requirements.description: Primary work surface for Kevin's AI Command Center. Features Kanban board with columns (Inbox, Assigned, In Progress, Review, Complete), drag-and-drop, task cards with assignee/priority/due date/tags, filtering, quick-add, and local JSON persistence.

requirements.name: Mission Control v3 - 3D Isometric Visualization (Agent World)
requirements.description: Three.js-powered isometric visualization of Kevin's virtual HQ where agents appear as voxel characters. Includes agent positioning, state visualization (idle/working/reporting/waiting), clickable agent profiles, task direction, sub-agent visualization, and ambient animation.

requirements.name: Mission Control v3 - Health Monitoring Integration
requirements.description: Integrated health monitoring panel for Kevin's wearables and genetic context. Integrates Oura Ring (sleep/readiness/HRV), OMI Device (voice/memory), supplement tracking, exercise logging with 48h recovery, genetic context (AMPD1/AGK variants), and CK trending alerts.

requirements.name: Mission Control v3 - Agent Activity Feed
requirements.description: Real-time feed showing agent activity with live logs, session links, per-agent cost tracking, total API spend monitoring, sub-agent tree visualization, and filtering by agent/time-range/cost-threshold.

requirements.name: Mission Control v3 - Agent Profile System
requirements.description: Foundational agent identity system with full profile data model (name, bio, role, tier, model, character type, voice, color, avatar). Includes Kevin's special owner profile, Executive Board (Groot, Jimmy T, CEO, Dr. Al, Dirty Bird), and sub-agent auto-creation with persistence.

requirements.name: Mission Control v3 - Rooms & Locations System
requirements.description: Room system for 3D Agent World with named locations, boundaries, labels, and agent navigation. Agents walk to rooms when assigned work, supporting hierarchical organization and location-based task assignment.

requirements.name: DX Dashboard - Shell v2 Visual Polish & Routing
requirements.description: Shell v2 build phase with visual polish and routing infrastructure. Includes custom icon integration (8 platform icons with theme-aware swapping), leather texture header effect, saddle stitching, dropdown glass morphism, fade-slide animations, 'Coming Soon' badges, instant theme switching, and page routing with 23 page stubs.

requirements.name: DX1 Home - Personal Hub Interface
requirements.description: Personal hub interface for DX1 platform providing navigation, theme switching, and connected services. Serves as landing page with groot-nav.js integration and responsive design.

requirements.name: Agent Roster - Agent Registration & Management Service
requirements.description: Node.js service for managing agent registration and roster. Provides API endpoints for agent lifecycle management, profile storage, and agent directory services supporting Mission Control v3 architecture.

requirements.name: Clipboard Manager - Shared Data Exchange Service
requirements.description: Service for managing shared clipboard data across agents and services. Enables inter-agent data exchange and temporary data storage for agent-to-agent communication.

requirements.name: Central Command & Mission Control v3 - Code Audit & Quality
requirements.description: Comprehensive code quality audit identifying NextAuth/iron-session migration tasks, SQL injection review, dead import detection, and cross-codebase consistency verification. Includes build test results and deprecation recommendations.

requirements.name: Agent-Based Health Research Management
requirements.description: Dr. Al agent autonomously manages health research tasks including genetic variant research (AMPD1, AGK), rhabdomyolysis root cause analysis, supplement optimization, and CK monitoring protocol development with weekly health status reviews.

requirements.name: Multi-Specialist Coordination Framework
requirements.description: System enabling agent hierarchy with core sub-agents (Neurologist, Geneticist, Nutritionist) and on-demand specialist spawning (Cardiologist, Rheumatologist, Endocrinologist, Psychedelic Researcher, etc.) with coordinated briefing and reporting.

requirements.name: Evidence-Based Decision Making with Exploratory Options
requirements.description: Agent capability to balance evidence-based interventions when available with exploratory/unconventional approaches (alternative medicine, emerging treatments) based on user preferences.

requirements.name: Legal Risk Assessment & Monitoring
requirements.description: Continuous monitoring for legal and compliance risks including contract review, regulatory changes, IP protection status, and malpractice threat detection with automated escalation.

requirements.name: Litigation & Evidence Management
requirements.description: Capability to handle complex litigation including trial evidence strategy, expert witness coordination, timeline reconstruction, and federal court procedures with documented audit trail.

requirements.name: Specialized Legal Sub-Agent Network
requirements.description: On-demand legal specialist coordination including IP attorneys, corporate law specialists, tech/crypto law experts, employment law, and malpractice monitoring with integrated knowledge base.

requirements.name: Ethical & Compliance Posture Management
requirements.description: Governance documentation for agent permissions, ethical review of automation decisions, and compliance verification to ensure company meets trust commitments in regulated domains.

requirements.name: Product Management & Feature Specification System
requirements.description: Dedicated product management capability for customer-to-roadmap pipeline including user research synthesis, feature prioritization, acceptance criteria definition, and roadmap sequencing to prevent escalation bottlenecks.

requirements.name: Go-To-Market & First Customer Acquisition
requirements.description: GTM infrastructure for first customer acquisition including outbound targeting, demo scheduling, objection handling, initial onboarding, and pipeline management to meet Day 60 customer target.

requirements.name: Observability Dashboard Design & UX
requirements.description: Trust-sensitive human interface for reading and understanding agent actions and rationale in the observability dashboard to establish first-impression credibility with customers.

requirements.name: Governance Documentation & Agent Permission Framework
requirements.description: Structured governance documentation defining agent permissions, authorization boundaries, and escalation thresholds to establish ethical and legal compliance posture.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: KanbanBoard — Task cards use text-[10px] font too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-001: Mobile Fix: KanbanBoard — Task cards use text-[10px] font too small for mobile. Category: defect. In KanbanBoard.tsx, task cards use `text-[10px]` for priority badges, assignee tags, and date indicators. These are too small for mobile screens (320-428px). Additionally, the stats row at the top (`flex gap-3 text-xs text-mc-muted`) has no wrapping and may overflow horizontally on narrow screens. Fix: Increase font sizes to `text-xs` minimum (12px) and ensure stats row wraps properly with `flex-wrap`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: IssuesLog — Table lacks overflow handling and uses too-small fonts
requirements.description: Issue ISS-002: Mobile Fix: IssuesLog — Table lacks overflow handling and uses too-small fonts. Category: defect. In IssuesLog.tsx: (1) The issues table container doesn't have `overflow-x-auto`, so on mobile it will overflow the viewport and cause horizontal scroll issues. (2) Uses `text-[10px]` for issue IDs, category labels, and action buttons - too small for mobile. (3) Grid layout `grid-cols-4` in detail view has no responsive breakpoint - breaks on narrow screens. Fix: Add `overflow-x-auto` to table container, change `text-[10px]` to `text-xs`, change grid to `grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-4`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: FeaturesPanel — Uses text-[9px] and text-[10px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-003: Mobile Fix: FeaturesPanel — Uses text-[9px] and text-[10px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In FeaturesPanel.tsx, multiple elements use `text-[9px]` and `text-[10px]` including: status badges in expanded feature list, tech stack tags, and AI ideas section. These are too small for mobile touch targets. Fix: Increase all `text-[9px]` to `text-xs` (12px) and `text-[10px]` to `text-xs` or `text-sm`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: SettingsPanel — Uses text-[9px] and text-[10px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-004: Mobile Fix: SettingsPanel — Uses text-[9px] and text-[10px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In SettingsPanel.tsx: (1) Uses `text-[9px]` for tier badges, status indicators in models tab, and character type IDs. (2) Uses `text-[10px]` throughout API keys section, tier configuration, and model cards. These are too small for mobile. Fix: Increase `text-[9px]` to `text-xs` and `text-[10px]` to `text-xs` minimum.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: HealthPanel — Uses text-[10px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-005: Mobile Fix: HealthPanel — Uses text-[10px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In HealthPanel.tsx: (1) ScoreRing labels use `text-[10px]`. (2) MetricRow uses `text-xs` for labels but this is fine. (3) Sleep stages and activity timeline labels use `text-[10px]`. (4) Genetic context section uses `text-[10px]`. These are too small for mobile. Fix: Change all `text-[10px]` to `text-xs`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: ChatRoom — Uses text-[10px] and text-[11px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-006: Mobile Fix: ChatRoom — Uses text-[10px] and text-[11px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In ChatRoom.tsx: (1) Room activity preview uses `text-[11px]` for message previews. (2) Last message timestamps use `text-[10px]`. (3) Agent count uses `text-[10px]`. These are too small for mobile. Fix: Change `text-[11px]` to `text-xs` and `text-[10px]` to `text-xs`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: AgentsSidebar — Uses text-[8px] and text-[9px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-007: Mobile Fix: AgentsSidebar — Uses text-[8px] and text-[9px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In AgentsSidebar.tsx: (1) Tier badges use `text-[8px]` font size. (2) Agent role/tasks use `text-[10px]`. (3) Group labels use `text-[9px]`. These are far too small for mobile. Fix: Change `text-[8px]` to `text-xs`, `text-[9px]` to `text-xs`, and `text-[10px]` to `text-xs`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: TradingDashboard — Uses text-[10px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-008: Mobile Fix: TradingDashboard — Uses text-[10px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In TradingDashboard.tsx: (1) Analytics stat cards use `text-[10px]` for labels. (2) Bar chart labels use `text-[10px]`. (3) Various table headers and data cells use `text-xs` which is acceptable, but some `text-[10px]` exist in trade rationale columns. Fix: Change all `text-[10px]` to `text-xs`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: GlobalChat — Draggable window uses mouse events (no touch support)
requirements.description: Issue ISS-009: Mobile Fix: GlobalChat — Draggable window uses mouse events (no touch support). Category: defect. In GlobalChat.tsx: (1) The floating chat window uses `onMouseDown`, `mousemove`, `mouseup` events for dragging and resizing - these don't work on touch devices. (2) Initial position is calculated as `window.innerWidth - 440` which will place the chat off-screen on mobile devices (typically 320-428px wide). (3) Fixed size of 420x500 doesn't adapt to mobile screens. Fix: Add touch event handlers (`touchstart`, `touchmove`, `touchend`), calculate responsive initial position, and use responsive sizing with `max-w-[90vw] max-h-[80vh]` on mobile.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: ProjectProfile — Likely has font size issues similar to other components
requirements.description: Issue ISS-010: Mobile Fix: ProjectProfile — Likely has font size issues similar to other components. Category: defect. ProjectProfile.tsx is a large component (92KB). Based on patterns seen in other components, it likely uses `text-[10px]` and `text-[9px]` throughout. Need to audit and fix all small font sizes. This component has persistent chat at bottom which needs mobile testing. Fix: Audit all font sizes, change any `text-[9px]` to `text-xs` and `text-[10px]` to minimum `text-xs`.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: ReportsPanel — Uses text-[10px] too small for mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-011: Mobile Fix: ReportsPanel — Uses text-[10px] too small for mobile. Category: defect. In ReportsPanel.tsx: (1) Table headers and cells use small fonts. (2) Quick reports section uses `text-[10px]` for labels. (3) Status badges and priority ranks use `text-[9px]`. Fix: Change all small fonts to `text-xs` minimum.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: KanbanBoard — grid-cols-5 without mobile breakpoint causes overflow
requirements.description: Issue ISS-012: Mobile Fix: KanbanBoard — grid-cols-5 without mobile breakpoint causes overflow. Category: defect. In KanbanBoard.tsx, the columns use `grid grid-cols-5` which doesn't have responsive breakpoints. On mobile, this causes overflow. While there is a mobile column switcher, the initial render may cause issues. Additionally, the stats summary row (`flex gap-3 text-xs text-mc-muted`) lacks `flex-wrap` which causes horizontal overflow. Fix: Ensure `grid-cols-5` only applies on larger screens with `grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-5` and add `flex-wrap` to stats row.

requirements.name: Mobile Fix: WorldTab — Hardcoded height causes overflow on mobile
requirements.description: Issue ISS-013: Mobile Fix: WorldTab — Hardcoded height causes overflow on mobile. Category: defect. In WorldTab.tsx, the main container uses `style={{ height: 'calc(100vh - 120px)', overflow: 'hidden' }}` which is a fixed calculation that may not work correctly on mobile browsers with different viewport behaviors (e.g., mobile address bars). Additionally, the iframe needs to be tested on mobile touch. Fix: Use responsive height calculation or percentage-based heights that work across devices.

requirements.name: Invoice & Client Manager
requirements.description: Mission Control project: Invoice & Client Manager. Professional invoicing and client management SaaS app for freelancers and agencies. Features client directory, invoice builder with AI formatting, payment tracking, and recurring invoices.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: Cyber Heist Game
requirements.description: Mission Control project: Cyber Heist Game. Strategic puzzle/stealth game where players plan and execute digital heists on AI-generated corporate targets. Features team assembly, planning phase, execution with dice rolls, leaderboards, and seasonal passes.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: Course Builder App
requirements.description: Mission Control project: Course Builder App. AI-powered online course creation platform for knowledge creators. Features drag-to-reorder modules/lessons, AI-powered outline/content/quiz generation, student tracking, revenue calculator, and landing page generator.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: AI Dungeon Master Game
requirements.description: Mission Control project: AI Dungeon Master Game. Text-based RPG adventure game where AI generates story, choices, and consequences. Features character creation, campaign themes (fantasy/sci-fi/horror/apocalypse/pirate), turn-based combat, inventory system, achievements, and save/load functionality.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: Pitch Deck Builder App
requirements.description: Mission Control project: Pitch Deck Builder App. AI-powered pitch deck creation tool for entrepreneurs and startups. Features step-by-step wizard, template library (seed/series-a/SaaS/marketplace/hardware/biotech), AI content generation, investor research, practice mode, and markdown export.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: Debate Arena Game
requirements.description: Mission Control project: Debate Arena Game. Multiplayer debate game where AI judges your persuasion skills. Challenge AI opponents of escalating difficulty, compete in tournaments, build streaks, and master argumentation.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: Habit Forge App
requirements.description: Mission Control project: Habit Forge App. Gamified habit tracking app with AI coaching. Track habits across categories, build streaks, level up, earn XP, receive personalized AI coaching, and transform your life.. Phases: Phase 1

requirements.name: MaxTarget Marketing Dashboard Overhaul
requirements.description: Mission Control project: MaxTarget Marketing Dashboard Overhaul. Complete overhaul of MaxTarget Marketing Dashboard — transform from placeholder UI with AI text generation to functional marketing suite with real CRUD operations, data models, AI-powered insights, and actionable workflows. Comparable to HubSpot, Mailchimp, Semrush, Hootsuite, and Google Ads.. Phases: Phase 1: Foundation, Phase 2: Marketing Suite, Phase 3: Advanced Features, Phase 4: Enterprise

requirements.name: VR Mode — Mission Control
requirements.description: Mission Control project: VR Mode — Mission Control. Immersive 3D virtual command center for Mission Control. Transform the dashboard into a futuristic sci-fi VR environment with floating holographic panels for tasks, health, agents, and chat. Uses Three.js for 3D rendering with WebXR support for VR headsets.. Phases: Phase 1: Prototype, Phase 2: Data Integration, Phase 3: VR Support, Phase 4: Polish & Performance

requirements.name: System Maintenance
requirements.description: Mission Control project: System Maintenance. Centralized hub for all system maintenance: cost optimization, session cleanup, cron management, context health, backups, infrastructure upkeep.. Phases: Phase 1: Cost Optimization & Session Hygiene, Phase 2: Automated Maintenance Routines, Phase 3: Maintenance Dashboard Tab

requirements.name: Legacy Dashboard Retirement

Only MC v3 remains active.. Phases: Audit Complete, Data Extracted, MC v1 Removed, Portal v2 Removed, Verified Clean

requirements.description: Mission Control project: Mission Control Multi-User Authentication. Google OAuth SSO integration with per-user workspaces, onboarding flow, and role-based access control.
